questions.question: Are there significant anomalies or spikes in methane emissions for any country-sector combination in a given year?

analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap effectively visualizes anomalies or spikes across many categorical combinations (Country-Sector vs. Year). By encoding emission deviations (z-score or min-max normalized) by color, major anomalies (spikes) within each country-sector row stand out. This makes it easy to visually scan many combinations and years for significant spikes or outlier years, addressing the analytical focus across the full table. The structure (195 countries x 9 sectors) means a heatmap allows a dense, high-level overview while preserving the ability to inspect granular spikes.
analysis.target_insight: Which country-sector combinations saw major year-over-year emission spikes, pinpointed by visually prominent color shifts on specific years.

chart_encodings.color: Normalized deviation in emission (per country-sector row, per year)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Colorbar for emission anomaly scale; sort rows descending by max deviation; axis tick trimming for large row count

transformations: Unpivot year columns from '1990' to '2018' into a single 'Year' column with emissions as value
Group by ['Country', 'Sector'] to create a panel with emission time-series per combination
For each ['Country', 'Sector'], compute per-year z-score/deviation from its own mean to identify spikes
Reshape (pivot) so that rows are ['Country', 'Sector'], columns are 'Year', and values are normalized deviation
Sort rows by maximum emission deviation to highlight the most anomalous combinations
chart_suggestions.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\2.csv')
years = [str(y) for y in range(1990, 2019)]
df_melt = df.melt(id_vars=['Country','Sector'], value_vars=years, var_name='Year', value_name='Emissions')
df_melt['Emissions_z'] = df_melt.groupby(['Country','Sector'])['Emissions'].transform(lambda x: (x-x.mean())/x.std(ddof=0))
heatmap_data = df_melt.pivot_table(index=['Country','Sector'], columns='Year', values='Emissions_z')
heatmap_data = heatmap_data.loc[heatmap_data.max(axis=1).sort_values(ascending=False).index]
plt.figure(figsize=(16, 14))
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis']
im = plt.imshow(heatmap_data.values, aspect='auto', cmap=cmap, vmin=-3, vmax=3)
plt.colorbar(im, label='Emission spike z-score')
plt.xticks(np.arange(len(years)), years, rotation=90)
top_rows = heatmap_data.index[:30]
plt.yticks(np.arange(len(heatmap_data)), [f'{c} ({s})' for c,s in heatmap_data.index])
plt.title('Methane Emissions Anomalies\nCountry-Sector vs. Year (Normalized z-score)')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Country (Sector)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

analysis.chart_type: Line chart grid (facet per Sector, top countries, emission outlier highlight)
analysis.design_rationale: Faceted line charts offer focused, sector-level deep dives into the temporal evolution of emissions for top emitting countries in each sector. By plotting 5-8 major countries per sector, and marking spike years with markers (outlier highlight), this design shows whether spikes/anomalies happen in specific countries/sectors/years or are sector-wide phenomena. This also helps distinguish consistent emission patterns from sudden jumps, supporting the anomaly-detection goal while making sector differences clear.
analysis.target_insight: For each sector, which countries experienced dramatic emission spikes in certain years? Are they consistent or sudden? Are spikes concurrent among countries, or isolated?

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Facet by 'Sector'; highlight spike years by markers; legend for countries; alpha for spike markers

transformations: Unpivot year columns from '1990' to '2018' into a 'Year' column with emissions as value
For each 'Sector', select top 5 countries by average emissions for clearer lines
For each country-sector, compute z-score to determine spike years (abs(z-score) > 2 as spike threshold)
Prepare panel data: for each Sector, plot lines for each selected Country across years, mark years with spike
chart_suggestions.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\2.csv')
years = [str(y) for y in range(1990, 2019)]
df_melt = df.melt(id_vars=['Country','Sector'], value_vars=years, var_name='Year', value_name='Emissions')
df_melt['Emissions_z'] = df_melt.groupby(['Country','Sector'])['Emissions'].transform(lambda x: (x-x.mean())/x.std(ddof=0))
spikes = df_melt[np.abs(df_melt['Emissions_z'])>2]
sector_country_means = df_melt.groupby(['Sector','Country'])['Emissions'].mean().reset_index()
top_countries_per_sector = sector_country_means.groupby('Sector').apply(lambda x: x.nlargest(5,'Emissions'))
df_top = df_melt.merge(top_countries_per_sector[['Sector','Country']], on=['Sector','Country'], how='inner')
sectors = df_top['Sector'].unique()
fig, axes = plt.subplots(len(sectors), 1, figsize=(16, 4*len(sectors)), sharex=True)
if len(sectors)==1:
    axes = [axes]
for i, sector in enumerate(sectors):
    ax = axes[i]
    df_sector = df_top[df_top['Sector']==sector]
    countries = df_sector['Country'].unique()
    for country in countries:
        df_plot = df_sector[df_sector['Country']==country]
        ax.plot(df_plot['Year'], df_plot['Emissions'], label=country)
        spike_years = df_plot[np.abs(df_plot['Emissions_z'])>2]
        ax.scatter(spike_years['Year'], spike_years['Emissions'], color='red', marker='o', s=80, alpha=0.6, label=country+' spike' if len(spike_years)>0 else None)
    ax.set_title('Sector: '+str(sector))
    ax.set_ylabel('Methane Emissions (MTCO2e)')
    ax.legend(loc='upper left', fontsize=8)
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.suptitle('Methane Emissions Spikes by Country for Each Sector\n(Highlight: Years with >2 std dev anomaly)', y=1.03)
plt.show()

table_metadata.description: Data loaded from CSV file: 2.csv (Dataset: Methane Emissions Around The World (1990-2018))

fields.name: Country
fields.sample_values: Afghanistan, Albania, Algeria, Andorra, Angola, ..., Vietnam, World, Yemen, Zambia, Zimbabwe
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field identifies the country or global region (e.g., "World") for which methane emission data is recorded, corresponding to national boundaries in the environmental domain.

fields.name: Sector
fields.sample_values: Agriculture, Energy, Fugitive Emissions, Industrial Processes, Land-Use Change and Forestry, Other Fuel Combustion, Total excluding LUCF, Total including LUCF, Waste
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field categorizes the source or activity sectors contributing to methane emissions, such as agriculture, energy, industry, and waste, within global environmental and climate reporting contexts.

fields.name: Gas
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field identifies the type of greenhouse gas measured, specifically methane (CH4), in global emissions data.

fields.name: Unit
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field specifies that methane emissions data are measured in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent (MTCO2e), indicating standardized greenhouse gas emissions quantification for environmental and climate reporting.
